# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains empirical evidence about multi-agent LLM system failure patterns, including benchmark-level underperformance, a 14-mode failure taxonomy, annotator agreement results, automated annotation performance, intervention outcomes, and repeated limits of prompt/topology fixes.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-012
evidence_id: evidence-01
kind: result
citation: Cemri et al., 2025, Abstract
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.13657
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Performance gains from multi-agent systems on popular benchmarks remain minimal relative to single-agent frameworks.

## Source Excerpt

"Despite growing enthusiasm for Multi-Agent Systems (MAS), where multiple LLM agents collaborate to accomplish tasks, their performance gains across popular benchmarks remain minimal compared to single-agent frameworks."

## Note

The statement is benchmark-level and comparative; it does not quantify the gap in this sentence.
```

### file: evidence-02.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-012
evidence_id: evidence-02
kind: method
citation: Cemri et al., 2025, Abstract
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.13657
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The study analyzes five popular multi-agent frameworks across more than 150 tasks using six expert human annotators.

## Source Excerpt

"We analyze five popular MAS frameworks across over 150 tasks, involving six expert human annotators."

## Note

The abstract states "over 150 tasks"; later sections also describe 150+ traces.
```

### file: evidence-03.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-012
evidence_id: evidence-03
kind: result
citation: Cemri et al., 2025, Abstract
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.13657
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The study identifies 14 distinct multi-agent failure modes organized into 3 categories.

## Source Excerpt

"We identify 14 unique failure modes and propose a comprehensive taxonomy applicable to various MAS frameworks." "These fine-grained failure modes are organized into 3 categories: (i) specification and system design failures, (ii) inter-agent misalignment, and (iii) task verification and termination."

## Note

This is mechanism-relevant because it structures where failures arise, but it is a taxonomy of failures rather than a direct causal mechanism for any one benchmark result.
```

### file: evidence-04.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-012
evidence_id: evidence-04
kind: metric
citation: Cemri et al., 2025, Abstract
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.13657
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Three expert annotators reached Cohen's Kappa 0.88 while refining the taxonomy.

## Source Excerpt

"This taxonomy emerges iteratively from agreements among three expert annotators per study, achieving a Cohen’s Kappa score of 0.88."

## Note

This metric is reported for annotator agreement on the taxonomy development process.
```

### file: evidence-05.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-012
evidence_id: evidence-05
kind: metric
citation: Cemri et al., 2025, Introduction
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.13657
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The paper reports ChatDev correctness as low as 25%.

## Source Excerpt

"Our empirical analysis reveals that the correctness of the state-of-the-art (SOTA) open-source MAS, ChatDev (Qian et al., 2023), can be as low as 25%, as shown in Fig. 1."

## Note

This is a point estimate tied to the reported empirical analysis and referenced figure.
```

### file: evidence-06.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-012
evidence_id: evidence-06
kind: metric
citation: Cemri et al., 2025, Section 3.3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.13657
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The few-shot o1 LLM-as-a-judge annotator achieved 94% accuracy and Cohen's Kappa 0.77.

## Source Excerpt

"As we achieve an accuracy of 94% and a Cohen’s Kappa value of 77%, we deem that the LLM annotator, with in context examples provided, to be a reliable annotator."

## Note

Table 2 attributes these values to the few-shot o1 configuration.
```

### file: evidence-07.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-012
evidence_id: evidence-07
kind: result
citation: Cemri et al., 2025, Introduction
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.13657
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
Best-effort prompt and orchestration interventions improved ChatDev by 14% but did not eliminate failures.

## Source Excerpt

"Our case study with AG2 (Wu et al., 2024a) and ChatDev (Qian et al., 2023) reveals that although these interventions yield a +14% improvement for ChatDev, they do not resolve all failure cases."

## Note

This creates tension with the idea that simple prompt or orchestration fixes are sufficient; the source presents improvement and insufficiency together.
```

### file: evidence-08.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-012
evidence_id: evidence-08
kind: claim-support
citation: Cemri et al., 2025, Introduction
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.13657
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The paper states that improved multi-agent performance remained too low for real-world deployment.

## Source Excerpt

"Moreover, the improved performance remains insufficiently low for real-world deployment."

## Note

The source gives a deployment judgment but does not specify a deployment threshold in the captured text.
```

### file: evidence-09.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-012
evidence_id: evidence-09
kind: claim-support
citation: Cemri et al., 2025, Introduction
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.13657
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The authors argue MASFT reflects fundamental design flaws in multi-agent systems rather than being only an artifact of current frameworks.

## Source Excerpt

"These findings suggest that MASFT is not merely an artifact of existing multi-agent frameworks, but rather indicative of fundamental design flaws in MAS."

## Note

This is an interpretive claim grounded in the reported findings, not a direct experimental isolation of all possible causes.
```

### file: evidence-10.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-012
evidence_id: evidence-10
kind: claim-support
citation: Cemri et al., 2025, Introduction
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.13657
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The findings indicate many failures arise from inter-agent interaction challenges rather than individual-agent limitations.

## Source Excerpt

"Consistent with these theories, our findings indicate that many MAS failures arise from the challenges in inter-agent interactions rather than the limitations of individual agents."

## Note

This is mechanism-relevant because it locates failure pressure in interaction structure, but it remains a broad claim rather than a single demonstrated causal pathway.
```

### file: evidence-11.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-012
evidence_id: evidence-11
kind: result
citation: Cemri et al., 2025, Section 4.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.13657
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
No single top-level error category disproportionately dominates the observed failures.

## Source Excerpt

"We highlight that no single error category disproportionately dominates, demonstrating the diverse nature of failure occurrences and the robustness of the taxonomy used to classify them."

## Note

This describes the distributional pattern in the analyzed failures rather than giving exact category percentages in the captured text.
```

### file: evidence-12.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-012
evidence_id: evidence-12
kind: claim-support
citation: Cemri et al., 2025, Section 4.3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.13657
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The paper says inadequate verification was a major contributor to failure, but not all failures can be reduced to verification alone.

## Source Excerpt

"In our study, we focus on verification issues in cases where the system can effectively benefit from the results of the verification process." "This leads us to conclude that, while many issues may indeed be traced back to inadequate verification, not every problem can be solely attributed to this factor."

## Note

This evidence preserves an internal tension: verification matters strongly, but the source explicitly rejects verification as a complete explanation.
```

### file: evidence-13.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-012
evidence_id: evidence-13
kind: method
citation: Cemri et al., 2025, Section 4.4
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.13657
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The taxonomy excludes common LLM failures such as text repetition when they are not specific to multi-agent systems.

## Source Excerpt

"Even though we encountered some common LLM failure modes like text-repetition, we exclude them from MASFT as these issues do not pertain specifically to MAS, and can occur even in single-LLM call pipelines."

## Note

This is a scope-setting methodological choice for what counts as a MAS-specific failure mode.
```

### file: evidence-14.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-04-012
evidence_id: evidence-14
kind: metric
citation: Cemri et al., 2025, Table 4 / Section 6.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.13657
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
In the ChatDev case study, baseline ProgramDev accuracy was 25.0, improved-prompt accuracy was 34.4, and new-topology accuracy was 40.6.

## Source Excerpt

"Baseline 25.0" "Improved prompt 34.4" "New topology 40.6"

## Note

These results show measurable gains from intervention, but the gains are still described elsewhere in the paper as insufficient for resolving failures fully.
---

# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains empirical evidence about multi-agent LLM system failure patterns, including benchmark-level underperformance, a 14-mode failure taxonomy, annotator agreement results, automated annotation performance, intervention outcomes, and repeated limits of prompt/topology fixes.

Performance gains from multi-agent systems on popular benchmarks remain minimal relative to single-agent frameworks.

---

---
The study identifies 14 distinct multi-agent failure modes organized into 3 categories.

## Source Excerpt

"We identify 14 unique failure modes and propose a comprehensive taxonomy applicable to various MAS frameworks." "These fine-grained failure modes are organized into 3 categories: (i) specification and system design failures, (ii) inter-agent misalignment, and (iii) task verification and termination."

## Note

This is mechanism-relevant because it structures where failures arise, but it is a taxonomy of failures rather than a direct causal mechanism for any one benchmark result.
```
